title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: The conflict in the Middle East has closed the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting aluminum shipments to the U.S. from the region. China's primary aluminum output in January and February 2026 rose 3% year-over-year to 7.53 million metric tonnes, driven by improved profit margins of 7,879 Yuan per ton. Syrah Resources faces a potential termination of its offtake agreement with Tesla unless defaults are cured by an extended deadline of June 1.

title: Bahrain begins output cuts at world's biggest Aluminum Smelter
summary: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) has initiated a phased shutdown of three production lines, representing 19% of its 1.6 million tonnes annual capacity, to preserve raw materials amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. This exacerbates industry issues from West Asia conflicts, causing price surges to four-year highs. Other smelters like Qatar's face gas shortages and shipment halts.

title: Week in review and thoughts for the upcoming week of March 9, 2026
summary: Middle East conflicts have halted aluminum shipments from GCC producers, the second-largest supplier outside China, with Aluminium Bahrain and Qatalum declaring force majeure. European supplies are strained by additional outages in Mozambique, Iceland, and Russia. Aluminum premiums are soaring, with US Midwest at $1.12/pound and European duty-paid at $450/ton, amid bullish forecasts to $3,700/ton.

title: Aluminium prices surge, supplies to America and Europe threatened
summary: Aluminum prices are surging due to threats to supplies in America and Europe. The closure of Qatalum smelter in Qatar from gas disruptions heightens concerns over global aluminum supply shortages.

title: Conglin Aluminum Technology Expands Global Aluminum Solutions with Advanced Manufacturing and Precision Engineering
summary: Conglin Aluminum Technology is expanding its aluminum extrusion, precision machining, and customized product development to meet global demand for lightweight, durable, and sustainable materials in transportation, construction, and other sectors. The company emphasizes sustainability through recyclable aluminum, reduced material waste, and lightweight solutions that cut vehicle weight by up to 400 kg, lowering energy consumption and emissions. It has earned recognition including the China Patent Gold Award and leads in national standards formulation.

title: Constellium Releases 2025 Sustainability Report, Shaping the Future of Sustainable Aluminum
summary: Constellium published its 2025 Sustainability Report, highlighting a 16% reduction in overall emissions intensity (scopes 1, 2, and 3) compared to 2024, driven by increased recycling and renewable energy procurement. Scrap input reached 47% of total metal input, up 13% from 2024, thanks to a new recycling center ramp-up. The company advanced low-carbon alloys, closed-loop partnerships, and met sustainability-linked bond targets while improving safety and product circularity.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduces Innovation Awards in categories including Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing to celebrate breakthroughs reducing environmental impact and enhancing efficiency. Exhibitors can submit implemented solutions or prototypes from the past 48 months starting in May, with winners announced at the event after industry voting. The awards aim to make innovations visible, inspire progress, and influence policymakers.

title: ASI certifies Lizhong Group against Performance Standard V3
summary: Lizhong Group achieved ASI Performance Standard V3 (2022) Certification for producing aluminum alloy ingots and cast aluminum alloy liquid at its Guangdong Longda Aluminum Industry Co., Ltd. facility in China. This certification underscores the company's commitment to sustainable practices in aluminum production. It aligns with industry efforts toward circularity and responsible manufacturing.

title: Aluminium-Free High-Barrier Films Market to Reach USD 3.82 Billion by 2035 as Sustainable Packaging Replaces Foil-Based Structures
summary: The aluminium-free high-barrier films market is growing due to innovations like recyclable mono-material films and advanced coatings that replace aluminum foil, appealing to eco-conscious consumers and reducing environmental impact. Companies such as Constantia Flexibles invested in MDO lines for high-barrier protection without metal, while Jindal Poly Films expands production of sustainable alternatives. Bio-derived and compostable materials further support lower reliance on fossil-based plastics.

title: Future of Steel: Trends, Technology & Sustainability 2026
summary: This article examines upcoming steel industry trends, focusing on the convergence of direct reduced iron technology, hydrogen-based operations, and steel scrap recycling systems. These technologies are creating a robust circular steel economy with minimal waste and optimized resource consumption aligned with global climate objectives.

title: Tata Steel Nederland Opens New Production Line for Sustainable Food Packaging
summary: Tata Steel Nederland commissioned a new production line using innovative TCCT (Trivalent Chromium Coating Technology) for sustainable packaging steel production. The technology complies with future legislation, improves food safety standards, and reduces costs by eliminating the need for additional lacquer layers.

title: Advancing 100% Recycled Steel for Sustainable Manufacturing
summary: Electrolux Professional is participating in the CiSMA project to validate 100% recycled steel in professional laundry equipment using Electric Arc Furnace technology. This approach reduces CO₂ emissions by more than 75% compared to traditional blast furnaces while addressing scrap quality challenges through advanced analysis and automatic separation.

title: Electric Arc Furnace Market to Surge Globally as Steel Industry Embraces Green Solutions
summary: This market report highlights how EAF technology is driving global steel industry transformation through scrap recycling, plant modernization, and sustainability initiatives. Notable developments include Tata Steel U.K.'s $1.6 billion investment in a new EAF facility expected to reduce carbon emissions by approximately 90%.

title: Slower production growth will push copper market to deficit in 2026
summary: The International Copper Study Group (ICSG) revised its 2026 copper market forecast to a deficit of 150,000 metric tons due to slower refined production growth of 0.9% (down from expected 3.4% in 2025). Recent mine disruptions in Indonesia, Chile, and Congo have prompted downward revisions, though the ICSG expects mine output to rise 2.3% in 2026 with recovery from expanded capacity and improved output from key producing regions.

title: Copper price tops $12,000 for first time on tariffs, supply woes
summary: Copper prices exceeded $12,000 per tonne for the first time on record, driven by mine disruptions across the Americas, Africa, and Asia combined with tariff concerns and rising demand from electrification and AI infrastructure. BloombergNEF forecasts the copper market could slip into deficit as early as 2026, with potential deficits reaching 19 million tonnes by 2050 without major investment in new mines and recycling.

title: Indonesia nickel processing utilization to drop amid lower nickel ore
summary: Indonesia’s nickel industry group FINI stated that 2026 nickel processing production capacity utilization could drop to 70-75% from 90% last year due to a lower nickel ore production quota of 260-270 million metric tons. The country's nickel processing capacity is about 2.7 million tons for RKEF and HPAL smelters, requiring 340-350 million wet metric tons of ore. Nickel ore imports are expected to surge to 50 million tons, mainly from the Philippines.

title: Nickel Pulls Back After Powerful Year-End Rally
summary: Nickel prices are extending their drop amid lack of detail on Indonesia's output cuts, following a powerful year-end rally. Satellite data showed 1.2 million tonnes of January smelter capacity outside China went idle, adding pressure on prices. The article discusses market forces reshaping nickel supply dynamics.

title: How nickel mining will be essential for powering the clean energy transition
summary: Nickel plays a crucial role in lithium-ion batteries for the clean energy transition due to its unique properties. The industry is reducing its carbon footprint, with member companies cutting GHG emissions by 9% and committing to further reductions. High-efficiency nickel recycling, at 68%, supports a circular economy by re-entering products like stainless steel.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: Recent articles highlight innovations like NOVONIX’s all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology, reducing costs and environmental impact of nickel-based cathodes. Nickel-containing stainless steel aids green hydrogen and cellulosic ethanol production for sustainability. Nickel is sustainable across its value chain if all actors take responsibility, with sufficient supply for EV transition.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices face volatility in 2026 due to oversupply and rising EV demand, with battery-grade nickel demand tripling by 2030. Alaska Energy Metals’ Nikolai Project, the largest US nickel resource, advances via FAST-41 for infrastructure, aligning with US goals for domestic critical minerals supply.

title: High Nickel Ternary Material Market's Technological Evolution
summary: High nickel ternary materials drive EV battery advancements with higher energy density, stability via doping/coating, and AI-optimized discovery. Emphasis on sustainable sourcing reduces cobalt reliance, with cobalt-free options mitigating supply risks. Developments include Umicore’s expansion and Ronbay’s NCM90 enhancements.

title: Tenaris awarded pipe supply contract for Sakarya gas field phase 3 in Turkey
summary: Tenaris has been awarded a contract to supply line pipe, casing, coating, and related services for the third phase of Turkey's offshore Sakarya gas field project operated by TPAO. The deal includes 22,700 mt of seamless pipes produced at the Dalmine mill in Italy for flowlines in a 62 km MEG pipeline, plus 87,800 mt of welded pipes from Brazil for a 180 km export pipeline, along with additional line pipe and casing.

title: Tenaris wins supply deal for third phase of Sakarya gas field project
summary: Tenaris will supply essential equipment and services for the third phase of the Sakarya gas field project in the Black Sea, operating in ultra-deep waters. This includes pipes produced at facilities such as the Dalmine mill in Italy.
